name: AWS Lambda
description: This app integrates with AWS Lambda to perform lambda functions

actions.name: Remove Permission
actions.description: Revokes function-use permission from an AWS service or another account

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: The name of the Lambda function, version, or alias. The following name formats are accepted: function name, function ARN, and partial ARN

parameters.name: statement_id
parameters.description: Statement identifier of the permission to remove

parameters.name: qualifier
parameters.description: Specify a version or alias to remove permissions to a published version of the function

parameters.name: revision_id
parameters.description: Only update the policy if the revision ID matches the ID that's specified. Use this option to avoid modifying a function policy that has changed since you last read it

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: The name of the AWS Lambda function, version, or alias. The following name formats are accepted: function name, function ARN, and partial ARN

parameters.name: invocation_type
parameters.description: Invocation type

parameters.name: log_type
parameters.description: Set to 'Tail' to include the execution log in the response

parameters.name: client_context
parameters.description: The JSON that you want to provide to your Lambda function in the context object

parameters.name: payload
parameters.description: The JSON that you want to provide to your Lambda function as input

parameters.name: qualifier
parameters.description: Specify a version or alias to invoke a published version of the function

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: List Functions
actions.description: List available AWS Lambda functions, with the version-specific configuration for each

parameters.name: function_version
parameters.description: Set to 'ALL' to include entries for all published versions of each function

parameters.name: next_token
parameters.description: Specify the pagination token returned by previous request to retrieve next page of results

parameters.name: max_items
parameters.description: A value between 1 and 50 to limit the number of functions to be returned

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials

actions.name: Add Permission
actions.description: Grants an AWS service or another account permission to use a function

parameters.name: function_name
parameters.description: The name of the Lambda function, version, or alias. The following name formats are accepted: function name, function ARN, and partial ARN

parameters.name: statement_id
parameters.description: A statement identifier that differentiates the statement from others in the same policy

parameters.name: action
parameters.description: The action that the principal can use on the function. For example, lambda:InvokeFunction or lambda:GetFunction

parameters.name: principal
parameters.description: The AWS service or account that invokes the function. If you specify a service, use SourceArn or SourceAccount to limit who can invoke the function through that service

parameters.name: source_arn
parameters.description: For AWS services, the ARN of the AWS resource that invokes the function. For example, an Amazon S3 bucket or Amazon SNS topic

parameters.name: source_account
parameters.description: For AWS services, the ID of the account that owns the resource. Use this instead of SourceArn to grant permission to resources that are owned by another account (for example, all of an account's Amazon S3 buckets). Or use it together with SourceArn to ensure that the resource is owned by the specified account. For example, an Amazon S3 bucket could be deleted by its owner and recreated by another account

parameters.name: event_source_token
parameters.description: For Alexa Smart Home functions, a token that must be supplied by the invoker

parameters.name: qualifier
parameters.description: Specify a version or alias to add permissions to a published version of the function

parameters.name: revision_id
parameters.description: Only accepts the function's $LATEST revision ID. Use this option to avoid modifying a function policy that has changed since you last read it

parameters.name: credentials
parameters.description: Assumed role credentials
